name: Avocado Terrine
recipeIngredient: 1 avocado, peeled, rubbed with lemon and sliced ½ inch thick
recipeInstructions:
1. As you cut avocados, drizzle lemon juice on them so they will not darken.
2. Mash the avocados in a bowl and add the mustard and Worcestershire sauce.
3. In a separate bowl, lightly whip the whipping cream until it is slightly loose (soft peaks).
4. Fold the whipped cream into the avocado mixture.
5. Place parchment paper in a standard bread pan, ensuring there is enough to cover the top of the filled terrine. Spray the parchment with release spray.
6. Fill the terrine with half of the mixture and refrigerate for 20 to 30 minutes.
7. Remove the terrine from the refrigerator. Slice the extra avocado and drizzle lemon juice on it to keep it from darkening. Place the slices on top of the chilled mixture.
8. Pour the remaining half of the avocado mixture on top, drizzle some lemon juice, and fold the parchment paper over.
9. Refrigerate for 35 to 40 minutes until set.
10. While the terrine is chilling, peel, devein, and barely poach the shrimp. Let them cool in the poaching liquid if possible. Set aside for garnish.
11. Prepare the sauce by roughly cutting tomatoes into bite-sized pieces and placing them in a small frying pan or saucepan. Cook slightly, add lemon juice and cream, and mix in basil leaves cut into strips. Quickly cook off some of the liquid until cooled to room temperature or below.
12. To unmold, place the terrine in warm water for 1 minute or less and unmold onto a platter.
13. Slice into ¾ inch thick pieces and arrange shrimp and basil on the plate with the terrine. Spoon some of the basil-rich sauce over the top.
